key_insight: A deep learning pipeline can automatically quantify DLBCL tumor microenvironment features and distinguish biologically meaningful differences between GCB and ABC/non-GCB phenotypes.
clinical_relevance: These microenvironment-based measurements could complement existing Hans-classifier workflows to improve DLBCL subtype stratification and risk assessment.
method: Used an unbiased deep learning approach on multiplexed tumor images from a DLBCL patient cohort.
Extracted features describing cell composition, morphology, and spatial organization within the tumor microenvironment.
Compared subtype-associated patterns, including immune-cell proportions and interactions with M2 macrophages.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the analysis identified increased immune cell proportions in ABC tumors and a preferential interaction with M2 macrophages, indicating subtype-specific microenvironment signals.
watch_item: The findings are based on retrospective image analysis and should be validated in larger, independent cohorts before clinical use.
paper_title: Characterization of DLBCL cell of origin-phenotypes based on tumor microenvironment features
